text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: for a field array whose resolver can report both a root-level error on the array itself (e.g. "needs at least N items") and nested per-index errors on individual items in the same validation pass, calling trigger() alongside a field-array action such as remove() can wipe out the nested per-index errors, leaving only (or none of) the root error behind.

Concretely: set up a form with useFieldArray where the resolver returns an errors object shaped like { test: { type: 'min', message: '...', 0: {...}, 2: {...} } } — a plain object that carries the root error's type/message directly on itself while also carrying nested errors keyed by numeric index (this is the shape useFieldArray's own internal effect leaves errors in after an array mutation). Trigger validation once — both the root error and the nested per-index errors render correctly. Now call a field-array mutator like remove(i) followed immediately by trigger('test') again. After this second validation pass, the previously-visible nested per-index error(s) disappear from formState.errors, even though the resolver is still reporting them and the indices have simply shifted after the removal.

Fix the library source so that when an existing field-array error is already a plain object carrying nested numeric-indexed errors (not a bare root-only error, and not already an array), it is not coerced/wrapped in a way that hides those nested indices — the root error info and the nested per-index errors must both remain independently readable from formState.errors after subsequent trigger() calls. Do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run the relevant tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx`. Fix the library source; do not just edit tests.

text: The failure is in `executeSchemaAndUpdateState`: named resolver validation treats every object error for a field-array as a root error and passes it through `convertToArrayPayload`. That wrapper turns a mixed object (`type/message` plus numeric keys) into an array, so the numeric item entries no longer live at the field-array’s indices. I’m checking the surrounding tests and types before making the smallest normalization change.

text: The existing helper is also used for built-in array-rule errors, so the fix must preserve its current behavior for bare root-only objects and arrays. I’ll add a narrow predicate for plain objects with numeric keys, retain that object in place, and add a regression test covering `trigger`, `remove`, then `trigger` with shifted item errors.

text: The source change is in place and keeps the existing mixed error object by reference only when numeric index keys are present; arrays and root-only errors still follow the existing conversion path. The focused Jest run is still executing.

The existing 72-test field-array suite passes. I’m adding the regression scenario to that suite so the exact mixed root-plus-index shape and the remove/trigger ordering stay covered, then I’ll rerun it and check formatting/type-level impact.

text: The regression test exposed an important second part of the behavior: preserving the old object alone keeps stale numeric indices after removal. The helper must retain the plain-object representation while replacing it with the resolver’s newly reported mixed object when that result also has numeric keys; that preserves the shifted entries and the root metadata.

{
  "name": "react-hook-form",
  "description": "Performant, flexible and extensible forms library for React Hooks",
  "version": "7.76.1",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs.js",

  "jsnext:main": "dist/index.esm.mjs",
  "source": "src/index.ts",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "exports": {
    "./package.json": "./package.json",

      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",

  "scripts": {

text:
Fixed the field-array error normalization bug.

Changes:
- Preserves mixed plain-object errors with numeric nested indices while updating root errors in [updateFieldArrayRootError.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts:18).
- Added regression coverage for `remove()` followed immediately by `trigger('test')` in [useFieldArray.test.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx:758).

Verification: focused suite passes, 73 tests; ESLint and formatting checks pass.
